as_boundary: Continue rejecting recipient non-init command-message inputs for standard-signal command tokens and multi-command conflicts; standard-signal recipient input is a resolved rejection boundary and standard-signal self-target command execution is preserved unsupported unless new source evidence replaces that boundary, while direct self-mailbox and completed self-target write-buffer execution are implemented by ADR-0161 and recipient write-buffer command-message execution is implemented by ADR-0169. The accepted current runtime behavior rejects only standard-signal command messages and multi-command conflicts.

implemented_claims.summary: AS now has a named transition claim and proof-certificate entry for the remaining recipient non-init command-message rejection boundary, covering standard-signal and multi-command conflict examples.

implemented_traces.summary: AS now has a schematic-linked trace that replays fixed-cell upstream standard-signal command-message rejection.
AS now has a schematic-linked trace that replays fixed-cell direct multi-command recipient rejection.

implemented_svgs.summary: AS now has a rendered SVG view checked exactly against the recipient non-init command-message rejection trace.
AS now has a rendered SVG view checked exactly against the multi-command recipient rejection trace.

implemented_evidence_bundles.summary: AS now has an integrated evidence bundle validating one recipient non-init rejection boundary across claim, proof, trace, render, and source-status layers while write-buffer recipient command messages have moved to ADR-0169 append execution.

implemented_source_statuses.summary: AS now has a source-status decision and runtime implementation for direct self-mailbox, completed self-target command-buffer, and recipient command-message write-buffer execution.

AS now has a source-status decision resolving recipient standard-signal command messages to the non-init rejection boundary and preserving self-target standard-signal command execution as unsupported unless new source evidence replaces that boundary.
AS now has a source-status decision selecting reject-and-clear for multiple simultaneous recipient command-message inputs.

formal_command_table.summary: The formal command table includes standard-signal as a command-buffer command.

legacy_special_message_exclusion.summary: The legacy special-message sets exclude standard-signal and treat standard input as a separate classification.

standard_signal_status.as_boundary: Continue rejecting standard-signal command-message input through the resolved recipient non-init boundary and preserve self-target standard-signal command tokens as unsupported unless new source evidence replaces the existing boundary.

legacy_divergences.summary: RAA dispatches write-buf-zero and write-buf-one through write-buf, which appends only when the buffer-full check permits it; input processing separately clears channels.
SEMSIM defines write-buf-zero and write-buf-one as appends, but stem-process-special-message wraps the selected operation in zero-hig and zero-buf, creating an unclear buffer-clearing boundary.
FSMSIM dispatches write-buf-zero and write-buf-one through primitive-eval and appends to the buffer while clearing the self mailbox and input channels.

write_buffer_status.as_boundary: Recipient write-buffer command-message input is implemented as append execution by ADR-0169. Direct self-mailbox and completed self-target command-buffer write-buffer execution remain implemented by ADR-0161.

multi_command_input_status.summary: multiple simultaneous command-message inputs are assigned the existing reject-and-clear boundary; AS does not prioritize or sequence them.
multi_command_input_status.as_boundary: ADR-0049 consumes exactly one single command-message input token. ADR-0059 selects rejection for two or more simultaneous command-message tokens.

allowed_next_slices: Review new standard-signal command-token source evidence before changing the ADR-0165 preserved unsupported boundary.
